title: Documentation, Demonstration, Independent Defense, and Reflective Transfer · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed review reconstructs documentation, demonstration, independent defense, and reflective transfer from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for public capstone dossier and independent defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
Demonstrate ordinary, changed, boundary, inaccessible-before-repair, failure, recovery, rollback, and stakeholder-outcome cases from the reviewed artifact.
Answer unseen causal, counterexample, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, data, operations, and transfer questions using inspectable evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reflective Transfer
steps.instruction:
review case — A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

Inspect the Reflective Transfer trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reflective Transfer.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reflective Transfer, keep Reflective Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Reflective Transfer, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reflective Transfer, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Reflective Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reflective Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Reflective Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Reflection is a chronological summary of completed work.

stimulus.title: Reflective Transfer case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Reflection is a chronological summary of completed work.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task. Artifact under review: public capstone dossier and independent defense.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Reflective Transfer, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Audience Documentation
steps.instruction:
Order the Audience Documentation evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Audience Documentation.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Audience Documentation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Audience Documentation, keep Audience Documentation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Audience Documentation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Reflective Transfer
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Reflective Transfer: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Reflective Transfer.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Reflective Transfer, keep Reflective Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review Reflective Transfer, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Reflective Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

For Record what required effort · Review Reflective Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Reflection is a chronological summary of completed work.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using compare and initial plus specific case evidence.
